_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 1955 batch)
_meta.note: VA LOCAL batch — Spotsylvania County Board of Supervisors 5-pack (the bug-flagged-from-prior-batch BoS members who all need the office-string 'County' prefix fix): (1) david-goosman R Berkeley District (sworn Jan 2026, term to Dec 2029; Calvary Baptist deacon/treasurer/music director — HIGH-BAR christian_liberty q0 TRUE rare on the local tier; voted YES on Jan 29 2026 7-0 2A Sanctuary reaffirmation; April 2026 voted YES on 73¢ rate that produces a 5¢ effective tax hike vs the 68¢ equalized — vote-bearing FALSE on econ q2 even though his rhetoric was restraint-leaning, parallel to Remick prior batch); (2) deborah-frazier D Salem District (35-yr Spotsy public-school educator/CAO, EdD Walden, Sylvannah Baptist member, first Black BoS member, lone D on 7-member board; HETERODOX-D CATCH — voted YES on Jan 28 2026 7-0 2A Sanctuary reaffirmation with on-record supportive statement 'wanted to make it clear that she's supporting the resolution'; CLEAN D-direction FALSE on econ q2 — voted NO on FY25 73.43¢ rate in 4-3 split because she wanted HIGHER 74¢ for schools, made substitute motion for higher rate); (3) drew-mullins R Courtland District (2026 Vice Chair under Hayes — NOT 2025 VC; 23-yr Navy SEAL Master Chief→LT 'Mustang' officer 14 deployments incl Iraq/Afghanistan/Somalia, BA History UMW '19, MA International Security Studies U Arizona; HARD TRUE self_defense q0+q1+refuse_state_overreach q4 — voted YES on 7-0 sanctuary reaffirmation with 'it's the board's job to ensure the constitution is being upheld' framing + publicly backed CA Mehaffey's May 2026 refusal to enforce VA gun ban; HARD TRUE econ q2 — twice voted to hold/lower rate, FY25 in 4-yes bloc on 73.43¢ vs 75.17¢ admin recommendation + FY26 maintaining 73.43¢); (4) gerald-childress R Chancellor District (USMC vet small-biz owner, prior Spotsy Planning Commission Chair, wife Kendall + 4 kids; same sanctuary YES + same FY25 73.43¢ YES bloc; ABSENT FY27 final tax-rate vote Apr 2026; pro-development data-center NO on the Feb 2026 SUP requirement); (5) jacob-lane R Livingston District (Spotsy HS '02 Luck Stone foreman + Post Oak Farm multigenerational black-angus cattle since '60s, wife Lyndsay + 3 kids; HIGHEST-BAR refuse_state_overreach q4 + self_defense q0+q1 — PERSONALLY INITIATED the Jan 29 2026 reaffirmation with the quoted line 'It is my intention to assure the residents of Livingston that we are still a Second Amendment sanctuary county, and we will be paying close attention to the bills that will be coming out of Richmond'; HARD TRUE econ q2 — same 4-yes FY25 bloc + FY26 rate-hold). NET BATCH: 19 TRUE + 2 FALSE + ~129 NULL across 150 evidence cells. OFFICE-STRING FIX applied to all 5 records — adds 'County' prefix so refine-records.py classify_office_tier regex matches local-tier per yakabouski precedent from prior batch. PARTY CONFIRMATIONS: all 5 already correctly labeled; Frazier first D Black supervisor in Spotsy history (Nov 2019 def Trampe 52.66/47.32; Nov 2023 re-elected 66.36% vs Searles). NO ONE ON NOV 2026 BALLOT — Goosman term ends Dec 2029, others Dec 2027. Profile-building substantial for Mullins (Navy SEAL bio) + Frazier (35-yr educator + EdD) + Goosman (Calvary Baptist lay leadership).

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Berkeley District

profile.religion: Baptist — member of Calvary Baptist Church (Spotsylvania, VA; independent Baptist congregation pastored by Justin Spradlin), where he serves in public lay leadership roles: deacon, treasurer, and music director.
profile.education: B.S. Computer Science & Engineering, University of Texas at Arlington; M.S. Information Technology, University of Maryland University College (UMUC).
profile.background: 21-year U.S. Air Force officer/engineer (retired); then DoD civilian engineer supporting the U.S. Marine Corps at Marine Corps Base Quantico, retired from federal service December 2024. Spotsylvania resident since 2002. Member of American Legion Post 320. Married to Barbara; five children and eight grandchildren. Prior county service: Spotsylvania Economic Development and Tourism Committee; Vice Chairman, Spotsylvania Economic Development Authority; Spotsylvania Transportation Committee. Won the Berkeley District seat in the Nov 4, 2025 three-way race with 47.47–47.65% over incumbent Kevin Marshall (R, 27.86–27.92%) and Nilofer Garza (23.91–24.16%) — ran against Marshall from the right/anti-data-center flank citing EDA conflict-of-interest concerns; sworn in January 2026; current four-year term ends December 2029 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot.

david-goosman.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania County BoS, Berkeley District, sworn Jan 2026, term ends Dec 2029, NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. PARTY VERIFIED R via campaign domain goosmangop.org + Facebook /GoosmanGOP/ + VPAP candidate page + Fredericksburg Free Press election coverage; Spotsy BoS races appear without party labels on the VA ballot but his GOP self-identification is unambiguous. RELIGION: deacon, treasurer, and music director at Calvary Baptist Church (independent Baptist, Spotsylvania, pastored by Justin Spradlin); FXBG Advance candidate questionnaire confirms Calvary Baptist membership and described lay-leadership roles. EDUCATION: BS Computer Science & Engineering U Texas Arlington + MS IT UMUC. BACKGROUND: 21-yr USAF officer/engineer + DoD civilian engineer at Quantico (retired Dec 2024), wife Barbara, 5 kids, 8 grandkids, American Legion 320. FACTION: anchored the new post-Nov-2025 anti-data-center BoS majority (Hayes-Lee Hill, Goosman, Yakabouski, Lane) — voted with Hayes for Vice Chair Mullins on Jan 13 2026 (6-0 with Mullins abstaining; Frazier perturbed at being passed over) and voted with the 4-3 majority Feb 2026 to require Special Use Permits for data centers. HARD TRUE christian_liberty q0 (HIGH BAR cleared): lay-leadership roles at Calvary Baptist (deacon + treasurer + music director) materially exceed bare-membership; deacon-level public-facing church leadership in an independent Baptist congregation is a defensible public profession of Christ in public office, parallel to the Butler-Barlow Suffolk Council UMC TRUE pattern from the prior Tidewater batch. HARD TRUE self_defense q0 + q1 + refuse_state_overreach q4: voted YES on the Jan 29, 2026 7-0 BoS resolution reaffirming Spotsylvania's 2A Sanctuary status against VA General Assembly gun-control proposals; resolution text explicitly states 'the board of supervisors hereby reaffirms and renews its declaration of 2019 that Spotsylvania County, Virginia is a Second Amendment sanctuary' — direct refusal to enforce unconstitutional state directives, parallel to the Yakabouski TRUE pattern from prior batch. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 (vote-bearing FALSE, mirrors Remick prior batch): voted YES Apr 7, 2026 on the FY27 73¢ real-estate rate which after reassessments amounts to a ~5-cent effective increase over the 68¢ equalized rate (i.e., a de facto tax hike); his own rhetoric was restraint-leaning ('working hard to figure out opportunities to get cost savings... not going to impact services deeper or put the county in a deeper hole, yet show some efficiencies') but the rubric prizes vote-bearing fiscal discipline and the vote produced a hike vs equalized — parallel to Remick FY26 meals-tax FALSE. NOTE he was NOT seated for the FY25 Apr 2024 73.43¢ vote (that was Kevin Marshall's seat). NOT scored public_justice q0: platform language 'retain and properly compensate those who protect and serve us' is generic budget-style; no FOP/PBA endorsement, no proactive back-the-blue proclamation sponsorship, no officer-plus-up statement located — fails the HIGH BAR per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty q1-q3 / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach q0-q3 / border_immigration / self_defense q2-q4 — no quote-bearing positions located in his ~5-month first BoS tenure despite extensive social-media + meeting-minutes review. OFFICE-STRING FIX: changed 'Board of Supervisors — Berkeley District' → 'County Board of Supervisors — Berkeley District' so the engine's classify_office_tier regex `county (commissioner|supervisor|judge|board)` matches local-tier (parallel to the Yakabouski fix from prior batch). 4 TRUE / 1 FALSE on cited cells = ~80% (sparse cell denominator) — coherent Baptist-deacon constitutional-conservative BoS first-termer whose distinctive features are public Christian lay leadership + 2A sanctuary reaffirmation + FY27 effective tax-hike. Recommend revisiting after 12 months on the board and any 2026-27 culture-issue BoS votes.

0.note: Member of Calvary Baptist Church (Spotsylvania, independent Baptist) serving as deacon, treasurer, and music director — public lay-leadership roles materially exceed bare membership and qualify as public profession of Christ in public office, parallel to the Butler-Barlow Suffolk-Council UMC TRUE pattern from prior Tidewater batch.

2.note: Voted YES Apr 7, 2026 on the FY27 73¢ real-estate tax rate which after reassessments amounts to a ~5-cent effective increase over the 68¢ equalized rate (de facto tax hike). His own rhetoric was restraint-leaning ('working hard to figure out opportunities to get cost savings... show some efficiencies') but the rubric prizes vote-bearing fiscal discipline. Parallel to Remick FY26 meals-tax FALSE — vote-bearing rubric-adverse on q2 (balanced budgets without tax hikes).

4.note: Voted YES on the Jan 29, 2026 7-0 BoS resolution reaffirming Spotsylvania's 2A Sanctuary status against pending Virginia General Assembly gun-control proposals; resolution text 'the board of supervisors hereby reaffirms and renews its declaration of 2019 that Spotsylvania County, Virginia is a Second Amendment sanctuary' — direct refusal to enforce unconstitutional state directives. Mirrors Yakabouski TRUE pattern from prior batch.

0.note: Voted YES on the Jan 29, 2026 7-0 2A Sanctuary reaffirmation — combined sanctuary-refusal posture against any future local gun-registry / gun-shop-zoning ordinances that would flow from Richmond gun-control mandates.

1.note: Same Jan 29, 2026 7-0 reaffirmation — explicit local-ordinance refusal of state red-flag/magazine/AWB-style mandates ('reaffirms and renews its declaration of 2019'); 2019 sanctuary covered the Northam-trifecta gun-control package including AWB and magazine limits.

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Salem District

profile.religion: Baptist — member of Sylvannah Baptist Church (8400 Courthouse Rd, Spotsylvania; pastored by Dennis A. Woodard, Sr.), an SBC-affiliated historically-Black Baptist congregation. Listed on her official county Salem District Supervisor bio.
profile.education: B.S. and Master of Education, South Carolina State University; M.A. in Administration & Supervision, Furman University; Doctorate (Ed.D./Ph.D.) in Administration and Educational Leadership, Walden University. Licensed Virginia K-12 superintendent.
profile.background: Born in South Carolina; Spotsylvania resident 20+ years. 35+ years as a public-school educator in Spotsylvania County Public Schools — longtime principal at Chancellor Middle School, currently Chief Academic Officer of SCPS. Wife, mother of three, grandmother. Serves on Rappahannock Area Office on Youth, Potomac and Rappahannock Transportation Commission (PRTC), Virginia Railway Express (VRE) Operations Board, and Spotsylvania Regional Hospital Board of Trustees. First African American and first Black woman ever elected to the Spotsylvania Board of Supervisors. Elected Nov 5, 2019, defeating two-term Republican incumbent Paul Trampe 2,371–2,130 (52.66%–47.32%); took office January 2020. Re-elected Nov 7, 2023 over Republican Roy Searles Jr. 66.36%–33.01%. Current term Jan 1, 2024 – Dec 31, 2027 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot.

deborah-frazier.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania County BoS, Salem District, took office Jan 2020 (Nov 2019 election), re-elected Nov 2023, current term ends Dec 31 2027 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. PARTY VERIFIED D via VPAP candidate page + 'Frazier for Spotsylvania County Supervisor Deborah' committee + Fredericksburg Free Press 'lone Democrat on the board' multiple references. Woodard Sr.) listed on county bio — bare-membership signal absent additional deacon/board/Sunday-school-teaching roles; does NOT clear the Butler-Barlow / Goosman HIGH-BAR christian_liberty q0 standard absent invocation-delivery or lay-leadership documentation. This is a striking heterodox-D signal — lone D on the board affirmatively backing a state-overreach refusal vote, parallel to no other D refinement in the VA local batch to date. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 (CLEAN D-DIRECTION FALSE — pro-spending fault line): voted NO in the 4-3 April 2024 FY25 budget that adopted the 73.43¢ real-estate rate, because she wanted a HIGHER rate to fund schools more; later in the FY26 cycle she made a substitute motion to advertise the rate at 74¢ (only Yakabouski seconded). Consistent pro-spending lone-D fault line on the rubric q2 'balanced budgets without tax hikes' cell. NOT scored public_justice q0: no AFFIRMATIVE pro-police advocacy located — no FOP/PBA endorsement, no officer-plus-up motion, no back-the-blue proclamation sponsorship; per HIGH BAR convention default to NULL absent disqualifying quote (Free Press also reports she publicly criticized a Qualified-Immunity-for-public-employees resolution Feb 2026, but that does not affirmatively disqualify her on q0 without a clearer anti-LE quote). NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty q0-q3 / election_integrity / border_immigration / self_defense q2-q4 — no quote-bearing positions located. The 2020 post-George-Floyd 'equality' proclamation Frazier praised was race/civil-rights-focused not LGBT-focused; biblical_marriage cells stay null absent a Pride/marriage vote. ABSTAINED Feb 2026 on redistricting-referendum protest letter + Jan 14 2026 data-center policy reversal (read prepared statement decrying being denied Vice Chair seat). Recommend revisiting after any 2026-27 culture-issue BoS votes and her 2027 re-election cycle.

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Courtland District (Vice Chairman)

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no specific church home, denomination, or faith statement located on his campaign site (drew4spotsy.com), county bio, NACo profile, or local press coverage reviewed.
profile.education: B.A. History, University of Mary Washington (2019); M.A. International Security Studies, University of Arizona (in progress at time of swearing-in Jan 2024).
profile.background: 23-year U.S. Navy SEAL career, including service with a Special Mission Unit. Enlisted, rose to Master Chief, then commissioned as a 'Mustang' SEAL officer, retiring as a Lieutenant. Over 14 combat deployments — Bosnia, Eastern Europe, Africa; combat tours in Iraq, Afghanistan, and Somalia. Post-service work for U.S. government agencies in operations and training; small-business owner. Spotsylvania resident since 2004; married, two children — one currently enrolled in Spotsylvania County Public Schools. Open-seat winner Nov 7, 2023 — defeated Michael Bush 54.8%–44.6% as the GOP-endorsed Republican nominee (incumbent David Ross did not seek reelection). Sworn in Jan 1, 2024; current term ends Dec 31, 2027 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Elected 2026 BoS Vice Chair on Jan 13, 2026 (6-0; Mullins abstaining) under Chair Lori Hayes (Lee Hill); was NOT 2025 Vice Chair (Hayes held that role under 2025 Chair Yakabouski).

drew-mullins.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania County BoS, Courtland District, sworn Jan 1 2024, term ends Dec 31 2027 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. PARTY VERIFIED R via Spotsylvania County Republican Committee June 2023 endorsement + $750 in-kind contribution from Spotsy GOP recorded on VPAP for 'Friends of Drew Mullins' + VPAP candidate page + Vote-USA Republican Party of Virginia listing. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed despite extensive review (campaign site, county bio, NACo profile, local press all silent). EDUCATION: BA History UMW 2019 + MA International Security Studies U Arizona. BACKGROUND: 23-yr Navy SEAL Master Chief → 'Mustang' LT officer with 14+ deployments incl Bosnia/Iraq/Afghanistan/Somalia; small-biz owner; Spotsy since 2004. LEADERSHIP ROLE CLARIFICATION: 2026 Vice Chair (elected Jan 13 2026 6-0 under Chair Hayes) — NOT 2025 VC (that was Hayes under 2025 Chair Yakabouski). HARD TRUE self_defense q0 + q1 + refuse_state_overreach q4: voted YES on the Jan 29 2026 7-0 BoS resolution reaffirming Spotsylvania's 2A Sanctuary status; quoted on the record 'it's the board's job to ensure the constitution is being upheld.' Additionally publicly backed Commonwealth's Attorney G. Ryan Mehaffey's May 2026 announcement that the office would not enforce Virginia's new gun ban (Daily Caller), reinforcing the constitutional-non-enforcement posture. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES with the 4-yes bloc on the FY25 April 2024 73.43¢ real-estate rate (vs admin's recommended 75.17¢ and advertised 78.17¢ — Mullins backed the LOWER rate); voted YES Apr 22 2025 on FY26 budget MAINTAINING 73.43¢; on the record during FY26 deliberations stated 'We're in the same mindset as you all' / 'We'll do the absolute best we can to take care of our fellow citizens.' Two consecutive rate-hold/lower votes meet the vote-bearing rubric q2 balanced-budget standard. NOT scored public_justice q0: campaign rhetoric 'first responders with necessary resources' is generic budget-style and no FOP/PBA endorsement, standalone back-the-blue proclamation, or officer-plus-up motion located; the 2A sanctuary vote and his explicit public alignment with the CA's gun-ban-refusal posture do imply LE-policy-friendly orientation but do not affirmatively clear the q0 HIGH BAR. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty (no disclosed faith) / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach q0-q3 / border_immigration / self_defense q2-q4 — no quote-bearing positions located despite extensive review. Brand is 'constitutional / pro-growth Navy SEAL,' not 'culture warrior' — score conservatively where evidence is absent. ALSO: pro-growth Dec 2025 4-3 YES on data-center design standards ('predictability... billions of dollars of investment'); cordial budget-cooperation posture with the school board (Apr 2025 'I hope we have more of that' and Mar 2026 'common ground' coverage) — does NOT line up with Twigg-bloc enforcer profile. OFFICE-STRING FIX applied (preserves Vice Chairman annotation). 4 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells — coherent constitutional-conservative pro-growth R Navy-SEAL BoS member with cleanest sanctuary-cluster + rate-hold profile in the 5-pack. Recommend revisiting after 2026-27 culture-issue votes.

2.note: Voted YES with the 4-yes bloc on the FY25 April 2024 73.43¢ real-estate rate (vs admin's recommended 75.17¢ and advertised 78.17¢ — backed the LOWER rate); voted YES Apr 22 2025 on FY26 budget MAINTAINING 73.43¢. Two consecutive rate-hold/lower votes meet the vote-bearing rubric q2 balanced-budget standard.

4.note: Voted YES on the Jan 29 2026 7-0 BoS resolution reaffirming Spotsylvania's 2A Sanctuary status; on-record statement 'it's the board's job to ensure the constitution is being upheld.' Subsequently publicly backed CA Mehaffey's May 2026 announcement refusing to enforce Virginia's new gun ban — reinforced constitutional-non-enforcement posture.

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Chancellor District

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no specific church home, denomination, or faith statement located on his campaign site (childress4chancellor.com) or county bio.
profile.education: Spotsylvania High School graduate; no further post-secondary education publicly disclosed.
profile.background: United States Marine Corps veteran (rank, dates, and deployment details not publicly disclosed). Small-business owner — roles at Aim Digital Media Inc., The Childress Agency Inc., and Childress Construction. 19+ year resident of the Chancellor District; wife Kendall; four children. Prior county service: Chairman of the Spotsylvania Planning Commission; Economic Development Authority; Transportation Committee; Government Military Affairs Council; Rappahannock Area YMCA board; Families of the Wounded Fund. Stated platform: 'limited government, individual responsibility, and fiscal conservatism... upholding the values of integrity, courage, and service' with a commitment to 'ensure law enforcement agencies have the resources they need to keep the community safe.' Won the open Chancellor seat Nov 7, 2023, defeating Michael Martin 60.31%–38.76% as the GOP-aligned Republican; sworn in Jan 1, 2024; current term ends Dec 31, 2027 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot.

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Livingston District

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no specific church home, denomination, or faith statement located on his campaign site (jake4livingston.com), county bio, news coverage, or Vote-USA.
profile.education: Spotsylvania High School, 2002 graduate; no further post-secondary education publicly disclosed.
profile.background: Born May 1984; lifelong Spotsylvania County resident. Family has operated Post Oak Farm, a small black-angus cattle operation in the Livingston District, since the late 1960s. Day-job: foreman at Luck Stone. Wife Lyndsay; three children (Marley, Jackson, Cole); two enrolled in Spotsylvania County Public Schools. Prior service: Spotsylvania County Planning Commission (appointed by prior Supervisor Barry K. Jett); Agricultural & Forestal Committee; Transportation Committee. Won the Feb 15, 2022 special election to fill Jett's seat (D. Oct 2021) over Democrat Raymond Bell by an 88-vote margin; then won the Nov 7, 2023 full-term general unopposed. Sworn in for full term Jan 1, 2024; current four-year term ends Dec 31, 2027 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot.

jacob-lane.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania County BoS, Livingston District, special-elected Feb 15 2022, Nov 7 2023 unopposed full term, current term ends Dec 31 2027 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HIGHEST-BAR TRUE refuse_state_overreach q4 + self_defense q0 + q1 (AFFIRMATIVE INITIATION beyond mere voting): personally raised the 2A Sanctuary reaffirmation at the Jan 29 2026 BoS meeting, and is quoted on the record: 'It is my intention to assure the residents of Livingston that we are still a Second Amendment sanctuary county, and we will be paying close attention to the bills that will be coming out of Richmond.' The following Tuesday the Board voted 7-0 to approve the new resolution. This is the highest-bar form of affirmative 2A-sanctuary advocacy in the entire VA local batch — initiation, not just voting along. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES with the 4-yes bloc on the FY25 April 2024 73.43¢ real-estate rate vs admin's recommended 75.17¢ — backed the LOWER rate; FY26 budget Apr 22 2025 maintained 73.43¢ (Lane in adopting majority). Two consecutive rate-hold/lower votes meet the vote-bearing rubric q2 standard. Lane's stated campaign cornerstone is 'fiscal responsibility of taxpayer funds' (Spotsy Wire endorsement). NOT scored public_justice q0: no FOP/PBA endorsement, no affirmative back-the-blue proclamation sponsorship, no officer-plus-up motion located; the 2A sanctuary statement is rights-of-citizens posture not LE-funding affirmation per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty (no disclosed faith) / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach q0-q3 / border_immigration / self_defense q2-q4 — no quote-bearing positions located despite review.
